story.iWant: to implement task complexity classification system
story.soThat: I can route tasks to appropriate models based on complexity level và optimize cost/quality trade-offs

subtasks.subtask: Define complexity levels: simple, medium, complex, very_complex

Document criteria for each complexity level: - simple: Short queries (<50 words), single intent, no complex reasoning (e.g., "What is X?", "Explain Y briefly") - medium: Moderate length (50-150 words), multiple intents, basic reasoning (e.g., "Explain X and Y", "Compare A with B") - complex: Long queries (150-300 words), complex reasoning, multi-step tasks (e.g., "Analyze X, compare with Y, and provide recommendations") - very_complex: Very long queries (>300 words), advanced reasoning, multi-model workflows (e.g., "Research X, create plan, execute, synthesize")
Create examples for each complexity level
Test complexity level definitions với sample tasks
Integrate với existing LLM calls for classification (if needed)
Test TaskClassifier class với sample tasks
Analyze task characteristics (length, keywords, intent, etc.)
Test classification logic với diverse task types
Log classification results (complexity level, confidence score)
Add classification metrics to monitoring dashboard
Ensure classification results can be used for model routing

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Complexity levels được defined (simple, medium, complex, very_complex)
Classification logic được implemented để analyze task complexity
Classification accuracy được tested và validated
Classification results được logged và monitored
Classification can be used for model routing decisions

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Epic Breakdown: LLM Optimization
doc.section: Epic 3: Multi-Model Orchestration - Story 3.1: Task Complexity Classification
doc.snippet: Story 3.1 defines requirements for implementing task complexity classification. Foundation for cost optimization (enables model routing). Effort: 3 hours. Quality Impact: ✅ ZERO (classification only, no quality impact). Prerequisites: Story 1.4 (Dual-mode architecture).

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Master Optimization Plan v1.1
doc.section: Phase 3: Multi-Model Orchestration - Task 3.1: Task Complexity Classification
doc.snippet: Task complexity classification requires defining complexity levels (simple, medium, complex, very_complex) và implementing classification logic (rule-based hoặc LLM-based). Classification accuracy target: >85%. Foundation for routing tasks to appropriate models.

doc.title: Research Guidance: Multi-Model Orchestration
doc.snippet: Task classification methods include rule-based (keywords, length, patterns) và LLM-based (use cheap model like gpt-4o-mini for classification). Complexity levels: simple, medium, complex, very_complex. Classification accuracy: >85% target.

file.reason: New file to create. Implement TaskClassifier class với classification logic, complexity level definitions, và integration với model router.
Agent execution flow. Need to integrate classification vào agent execution flow to classify task complexity before model selection.
Current model selection logic. Classification results will be used to override default model selection based on complexity.
LLM API call handler. Can be used for LLM-based classification (if using cheap model like gpt-4o-mini for classification).
Configuration management. Can add classification configuration (complexity thresholds, classification method) here.

constraint.description: Zero quality impact required. Classification only (no quality impact). Classification accuracy target: >85%.
Dual-mode architecture: Original mode (baseline) + Optimized mode (with orchestration). Feature flags allow switching and rollback.
Four complexity levels: simple (<50 words, single intent), medium (50-150 words, multiple intents), complex (150-300 words, multi-step), very_complex (>300 words, multi-model workflows).
Can use rule-based (keywords, length, patterns) hoặc LLM-based (use cheap model like gpt-4o-mini for classification). Rule-based is faster, LLM-based is more accurate.
Classification results feed into model router (Story 3.2). Must provide complexity level và confidence score for routing decisions.
Must not break existing LLM calls. Classification should be transparent to callers. Test with existing agent configurations.

interface.name: TaskClassifier class
interface.signature: class TaskClassifier: def __init__(self, classification_method: str = "rule-based"): ... async def classify(self, task: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: ... # Returns {"complexity": "simple|medium|complex|very_complex", "confidence": float}

interface.name: make_llm_api_call()
interface.signature: async def make_llm_api_call(messages: List[Dict[str, Any]], model_name: str, ...) -> Union[Dict[str, Any], AsyncGenerator, ModelResponse]

tests.standards: Testing standards include: Quality validation (classification accuracy >85%), Performance testing (classification latency, cost if using LLM), Integration testing (existing LLM calls, different models, agent configurations, model router integration). Use labeled dataset for accuracy validation.

test.idea: Unit test: Verify TaskClassifier class creation và initialization
Integration test: Test TaskClassifier class với sample tasks
Unit test: Verify complexity level definitions với criteria (simple, medium, complex, very_complex)
Integration test: Test complexity level definitions với sample tasks
Unit test: Verify classification algorithm (rule-based hoặc LLM-based)
Integration test: Test classification logic với diverse task types
Unit test: Verify classification accuracy với labeled dataset (target: >85%)
Integration test: Test classification accuracy với different task types
Unit test: Verify logging logic (complexity level, confidence score)
Integration test: Verify classification metrics are logged và appear in monitoring dashboard
Integration test: Test integration với model router (Story 3.2)
End-to-end test: Test classification → routing → execution flow
